weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Power + transition: explosive, comp-specific patterns to prime you for the peak block and the first test wave next week. Everything is fresh this week; read the cues, keep efforts sharp and short, and hold to 3 sets. Stay crisp — bank the freshness, don't add a set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · near-comp load, every rep sharp and fast
patterns.load: Your competition KB, or 1 size heavier, at competition box height
cues: Drive off the box fast on every rep to prime your comp speed.
Keep the volume modest and the quality high — no grinding reps.

patterns.name: Slow-Eccentric Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · 3-sec lower
patterns.load: Your competition KB held at the chest, at competition box height
cues: Lower yourself to the box over a slow three-count, then sit fully before you stand back up.
Keep the descent smooth and braced — don't drop or flop onto the box.

patterns.name: Paused Box Squat — Heavy
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · hold 2 sec on the box
patterns.load: Your competition KB 1 size heavier, held at the chest, at competition box height
cues: Sit all the way onto the box and hold a still two-count before you drive up.
Stay tight in the pause — keep your chest up and core braced, don't relax onto the box.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · 9 sharp throws at near-comp effort
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge low and explode up, throwing the ball up and back overhead with sharp, fast, full-effort intent.
Drive from a fast hip snap and let the arms whip the ball over your head behind you.

patterns.name: Dead-Stop KB Deadlift to Tall Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps
patterns.load: A KB about 35–53 lb you can pull fast — heavy enough to feel it, light enough to finish tall with speed
cues: From a dead stop on the floor, hinge down and pull the bell up fast, finishing tall with a hard glute squeeze.
Keep your chest up and back flat; stop the set if your back rounds.

patterns.name: Overhead D-Ball Toss for Height
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 reps
patterns.load: A 20 lb D-ball — heavier than the comp Dynamax ball, light enough to toss cleanly overhead
cues: Hinge low holding the 20 lb D-ball, then explode up and toss it straight up and slightly back, letting it land safely.
Step clear and let the ball land if it comes back toward you — never try to catch a falling heavy ball.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~50% of your max-rep test
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — rehearse competition speed and pace, driving each rep up sharp off the chest.
Stop the set when the bar slows or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Larsen Press with DBs
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps
patterns.load: Two DBs you can press for 6 sharp reps with your legs lifted off the floor
cues: Lie on the bench and lift your feet off the floor, then press two DBs up fast with only your upper body driving.
Keep your back flat on the bench and core tight for balance. Put your feet down and stop if you feel wobbly.

patterns.name: Single-Arm DB Floor-to-Lockout Punch
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each arm · fast punch up
patterns.load: One moderate DB you can punch up fast for 8 reps per arm
cues: Lie on the bench with one DB at your shoulder and punch it straight up to a fast full lockout, one arm at a time.
Lower under control to your shoulder. Stop the set when the punch loses its snap.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — near-comp feel, sharp setups, step down at 80%
cues: Get under the bar, pack the shoulders quickly, and hold quiet and tight.
Breathe slow; step down deliberately when grip begins to slip.

patterns.name: Mixed-Grip Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · switch which hand faces you each set
patterns.load: Pull-up bar, one palm facing you and one facing away · hold goal of 20–30 seconds
cues: Set one hand over and one hand under the bar to rehearse a comp-specific grip.
Step down under control the moment either hand fails.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Crush Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · feet on the floor, take weight off as your grip allows
patterns.load: Fat-grip attachments on the pull-up bar, feet lightly on the floor for support · hold goal of 20–30 seconds
cues: Clamp the fat-grip attachments and squeeze hard to overload the open-hand crush.
Set your feet back down and step out the moment your grip starts to fail.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec · comp pace
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Slam at full competition pace for 20 seconds, then rest — this rehearses race speed.
Keep each rep clean and full-reach even as your breathing climbs.

patterns.name: D-Ball Clean to Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps
patterns.load: A moderate D-ball, 10 lb (women) / 20 lb (men)
cues: Lift the ball from the floor to your chest, drive it overhead, then slam it straight down — one flow.
Reset on the floor and keep every rep crisp and fast.

patterns.name: Medicine Ball Overhead Toss for Height
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 8 lb (women) / 15 lb (men)
cues: Dip your knees and throw the ball as high straight up as you can, then step aside and let it land.
Drive through your legs and finish with a full overhead reach — the mirror of your slam.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · near-comp speed, dialed-in rhythm
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at near-comp speed with a locked-in rhythm
cues: Lock into the rhythm you'll use on comp day and keep every set identical.
Keep your jumps low and even so the pace never wobbles.

patterns.name: Loaded Standing Calf Raise — Double Reps
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps · full stretch at the bottom, full squeeze at the top
patterns.load: A 30–45 lb DB or KB held in one hand (other hand on a wall or rail), or two lighter DBs
cues: Rise all the way onto the balls of your feet, squeeze the top for a beat, then lower until you feel a full calf stretch.
Hold a wall or rail with your free hand so you stay steady through all twenty reps.

patterns.name: Sprint-Cadence Rope Skips
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 sec all-out singles
patterns.load: Your jump rope, all-out turnover for the short window
cues: Go all-out fast for the short window to rehearse top-end speed.
Rest fully between bursts so each one stays truly fast.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · near-comp effort, sharp and fast
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Run the full competition motion: arms back, hips snap, jump forward at near-max effort, stick both feet.
Reset completely at the line between jumps. Stop the set if you can't land it under control.

patterns.name: Standing Long Jump to Box
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · jump out and onto a low box
patterns.load: Bodyweight, jumping forward onto a knee-height sturdy box set a comfortable distance ahead
cues: Swing the arms back and jump forward and up to land both feet flat on the low box, sticking it softly.
Step down between reps and reset at the line. Set the box close enough that you land it cleanly every time.

patterns.name: Quick Ankle Pogo Bounce
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 bounces · stiff ankles, low and fast
patterns.load: Bodyweight, bouncing in place on both feet
cues: Bounce in place off the balls of both feet with nearly straight knees — let the ankles do all the springing.
Stay light and quiet. Hold a wall or rail for balance and stop if your contacts get flat or heavy.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 250m · near-comp 500m pace
patterns.load: Rower · right at your goal 500m split, shorter rest to feel race effort
cues: Hold your goal 500m split on every piece with only short rest between.
If you can't hold the split on the third piece, the first two were too fast — even them out.

patterns.name: Kettlebell Swing
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps
patterns.load: A KB you can snap to chest height explosively for 12 — moderate, not a grind
cues: Hinge at the hips and snap them forward to float the bell to chest height — power comes from the hips.
Keep your arms relaxed; let the hip drive throw the bell, don't lift it with your shoulders.

patterns.name: Renegade DB Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps each arm
patterns.load: Two moderate DBs in a hands-on-DB plank · light enough to keep your hips still
cues: Set up in a plank on two DBs, then row one DB to your hip while the other stays planted.
Drop to your knees if your hips can't stay steady; quality plank over heavy weight.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 150-yd shuttle (3 × 50 yd) · comp-pattern turns at near-race effort
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run at near-comp effort, sharp and competition-specific
cues: Run three 50s at near-race effort, rehearsing the exact down-and-back pattern of the event.
Hit each turn the way you will on comp day — controlled brake, firm plant, explosive exit.

patterns.name: Backpedal-to-Sprint Transition
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 transitions · rest 75 sec between sets · controlled change of direction
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Backpedal 5 yards under control, plant both feet, then turn and accelerate forward 10 yards.
Slow down before you plant — smooth, controlled turns, never a hard cut at speed.
Walk back and recover fully between reps so every transition stays sharp.

patterns.name: Rolling-Start Sprint
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 yards from a walking rolling start · build to a fast finish
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Start from a few easy walking steps, then smoothly accelerate into a 15-yard sprint — no sudden lurch.
Slow down gradually after the line; don't stop short or cut hard.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · near-comp speed, sharp (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Push the full 50 yards sharp and fast at comp weight to bridge into the peak block — this is rehearsal, not a timed test.
Hold about 45° with arms locked and push through your whole body — don't let your elbows bend.

patterns.name: Heavy Sled March (15 yd)
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 yards · short, powerful starts
patterns.load: Competition load + 25 lb per side (women: 2 × 50 lb / men: 2 × 70 lb) · short-distance strength overload
cues: Heavy and short — drive the sled just 15 yards with powerful, deliberate steps to train your starting strength.
Take big, forceful steps and stop the run if the sled stalls — quality over distance every time.

patterns.name: Tall-Kneeling Med Ball Chest Pass
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · max-effort explosive pushes
patterns.load: A med ball light enough to throw hard and fast, pushed forward to a partner or open space
cues: From a tall-kneeling position, push the ball forward as explosively as you can to train a fast horizontal push like the sled.
Reset your position between throws so each push is fresh and at full speed.
